item.title: DEBT: Replace nonisolated(unsafe) cache in SubscriptionManager with thread-safe pattern
item.intent: nonisolated(unsafe) bypasses the compiler's concurrency checking — Swift 6 strict mode will reject this, and the pattern can silently race
item.description: SubscriptionManager uses nonisolated(unsafe) for _cachedExpirationDate and _cachedReceiptJWS, updated on MainActor but read nonisolated. Replace with OSAllocatedUnfairLock<(Date?, String?)> for atomic read/write, or restructure so all access goes through the MainActor. Ensure the subscriptionReceiptJWS async property documents that it returns cached data, not a live fetch.
acceptanceCriteria: No nonisolated(unsafe) properties remain in SubscriptionManager
Cache reads and writes are atomic via OSAllocatedUnfairLock or actor isolation
Compiles cleanly under Swift 6 strict concurrency checking
subscriptionReceiptJWS behavior is unchanged but naming/docs clarify it returns cached data
